title: NAC Policy Compliance Trending
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" earliest=-30d
| eval outcome=case(match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)permit|access.accept"),"permit", match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)deny|reject"),"deny", true(),"other")
| timechart span=1d count by outcome
description: Daily percentage of authentications that receive “permit” vs “deny” vs “redirect” per authorization policy — trending for policy drift and rollout validation.
value: Daily percentage of authentications that receive “permit” vs “deny” vs “redirect” per authorization policy — trending for policy drift and rollout validation.
implementation: Normalize `authorization_result` from your ISE field set. Alert when `deny` share increases >2× baseline week-over-week.
visualization: Stacked area (outcomes over time), Line chart (deny rate), Single value (deny % today).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Authentication pattern spikes during ISE or RADIUS maintenance, certificate renewal windows, or RADIUS time-outs that affect many clients at once.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk_TA_cisco-ise.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ise:auth`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize `authorization_result` from your ISE field set. Alert when `deny` share increases >2× baseline week-over-week.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" earliest=-30d
| eval outcome=case(match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)permit|access.accept"),"permit", match(lower(authorization_result),"(?i)deny|reject"),"deny", true(),"other")
| timechart span=1d count by outcome
```

Understanding this SPL

**NAC Policy Compliance Trending** — Daily percentage of authentications that receive “permit” vs “deny” vs “redirect” per authorization policy — trending for policy drift and rollout validation.

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:auth`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk_TA_cisco-ise. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **outcome** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by outcome** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked area (outcomes over time), Line chart (deny rate), Single value (deny % today).
grandmaExplanation: We use network security tools to watch who accesses what, help prevent data leaks, enforce network isolation, and secure cloud and remote access.
